title: Swiss gay youth groups form a national umbrella body
description: Gay youth groups across Switzerland create the ISJS as a national umbrella coordinating their work; the body later widened its scope to lesbian youth as the ILSJS.

title: Youth group Albatros forms in St. Gallen
description: Albatros, a group for gay young people, is set up in St. Gallen, adding an eastern-Swiss node to the country's network of queer youth groups.

title: Three more cantonal AIDS support organizations are founded
description: AIDS-Hilfe organizations are established in the cantons of Zug, Aargau and Graubünden, extending the Swiss network of regional HIV/AIDS support and prevention bodies.

title: Liechtenstein establishes a state AIDS support service
description: Liechtenstein sets up a state-run AIDS-Hilfe, creating an official HIV/AIDS support body for the principality.

title: Basel shows the first Swiss exhibition on homosexuality
description: From 30 January to 6 March, the exhibition Männergeschichten documents gay life in Basel since 1930 — the first show on homosexuality staged in Switzerland.

title: Geneva holds its first Gaie Pride march
description: On 18 May, Geneva stages a pride event under the French name Gaie Pride, conceived at the same time as the eleventh national Christopher Street Day.

title: Switzerland observes World AIDS Day for the first time
description: The World AIDS Day proclaimed by the WHO for 1 December is marked in Switzerland for the first time.

title: Gay community groups organize in Neuchâtel
description: Homologay forms in Neuchâtel as a gay association paired with an anti-AIDS association, alongside a new les-bi-gay group; the independent Groupe Sida Neuchâtel starts the same year.

title: Dialogai in Geneva adds two member groups
description: The Geneva organization Dialogai gains a motorcycle club, Gaymotards, together with a Christian group, chrétiens et homosexuel-les (C+H).

title: Zurich marks twenty years of Stonewall with a culture festival
description: In June, a festival on Zurich's Kanzleiareal offers concerts, readings, film screenings and parties under a banner pairing twenty years of Stonewall with twenty days of culture.

title: The national Swiss lesbian organization LOS is founded in Basel
description: On 2 December, delegates meeting in Basel found the Lesbenorganisation Schweiz (LOS/OSL), giving lesbians in Switzerland a national political body.

title: The Stonewall foundation is set up in Basel
description: A foundation named after the Stonewall uprising is established in Basel to support gay community projects.

title: Gay students at Zurich's universities organize as zart&heftig
description: Students at the University of Zurich together with ETH found zart&heftig (z&h), a campus group for gay students.

title: Groupe Sida Jura is founded
description: An HIV/AIDS support group forms for the Jura region, extending community-run prevention work into another part of French-speaking Switzerland.

title: The magazine Anderschume/Kontiki takes a new name
description: In January, the Swiss gay magazine Anderschume/Kontiki restyles itself as AK Anderschume/Kontiki.

title: Zurich's gay men's chorus schmaz takes its name
description: On 8 February, the men's chorus schmaz adopts its name and begins performing in Zurich; a formal supporting association follows on 17 March 1991.

title: A newspaper uncovers the Bern police register of homosexuals
description: On 26 April, the Berner Zeitung reveals that the Bern cantonal police kept index cards on homosexuals; the resulting scandal ultimately forces the register's abolition.

title: Youth group Check Point starts in St. Gallen
description: In September, Check Point begins meeting as the youth branch of the St. Gallen organization HASG.

title: Lucerne gains the youth group why not
description: In October, the Lucerne organization HALU sets up why not, a group for gay young people.

title: Dialogai forms a youth branch in Geneva
description: A group for young gay men, Jeunes homosexuels / Jeunes Gay, forms within the Geneva organization Dialogai.

title: Gay Sport Basel is founded
description: Athletes in Basel start Gay Sport Basel (GSB), a club bringing gay men together through sport.

title: The regional group Barbarosa forms in Baden
description: Barbarosa begins organizing gay men in the Baden area of canton Aargau.

title: A cross-border leisure club for gay men starts in St. Gallen
description: The Bärenclub (BC) forms in St. Gallen as a leisure club drawing gay men from eastern Switzerland, Vorarlberg and southern Germany.

title: Youth group Ganymed forms in the St. Gallen Rhine Valley
description: Ganymed, a group for gay young people, is started in the Rhine Valley of canton St. Gallen.

title: Conference in Solothurn demands legal rights for same-sex couples
description: On 20–21 April, the Bern group HAB convenes a conference in Solothurn calling for clear legal recognition of lesbian and gay partnerships, an early push toward Swiss partnership law.

title: schmaz performs at the Swiss choral festival despite a naming ban
description: Although barred from carrying a word meaning gay in its official name, the Zurich men's chorus schmaz appears at the Swiss choral federation's singing festival in Lucerne on 26 May and receives a certificate of honor.

title: Switzerland marks its first Coming Out Day
description: On 11 October, the international Coming Out Day, observed in the United States since 1987, is held in Switzerland for the first time.

title: A gay and lesbian center opens in Basel
description: The SchLeZ center opens in Basel, giving the city's gay and lesbian groups a shared home.

title: Zurich project Herrmann begins supporting sex workers
description: An outreach project named Herrmann starts in Zurich, offering practical help to male sex workers.

title: The Bern singers SCHWUBS come together
description: In spring, gay singers in Bern form the ensemble SCHWUBS; a supporting association follows in 1993.

title: Swiss voters approve an equal age of consent in a national referendum
description: On 17 May, 73 percent of voters approve the revised penal code and military penal code: the age of consent becomes 16 for everyone, the ban on homosexual prostitution falls, and homosexual acts in the armed forces are no longer punishable.

title: Christian lesbian and gay groups hold their European meeting in Männedorf
description: From 28 to 31 May, the European Forum of Christian lesbian and gay groups holds its tenth annual meeting at the Boldern conference center in Männedorf, canton Zurich.

title: Switzerland's revised penal code takes effect
description: On 1 October, the penal-code revision approved in the May referendum enters into force, making the equal age of consent and the related reforms operative law.

title: The Swiss Bears club is founded
description: Bearded and bear-identified gay men across Switzerland organize as the Swiss Bears.

title: A school outreach project starts in French-speaking Switzerland
description: The projet école suisse romande begins bringing information about homosexuality into schools across the Romandie.

title: Bern groups open a community library
description: The Bern organization HAB sets up the Schwubliothek, a lending library of gay literature, in the anderLand center.

title: A lesbian and gay grassroots congregation forms in Basel
description: Worshippers at the open church Elisabethen in Basel establish a lesbian and gay grassroots congregation.

title: Homoaktion Basel forms
description: Activists in Basel start the campaign group Homoaktion Basel (HaK).

title: Gay tent city CAMP 92 is held at Herzberg
description: CAMP 92 assembles a tent city for gay men at Herzberg in canton Aargau.

title: A leather and motorcycle club starts in Zurich
description: The LMZ leather and motorcycle club forms in Zurich and soon merges with Loge 70, an older club of leathermen.

title: German-speaking Switzerland gets its first gay film festival
description: The Rapperswil film days screen a selection of gay films at the Schloss Cinema in Rapperswil, canton St. Gallen — the first festival of its kind in German-speaking Switzerland — and return annually until 2004.

title: Zurich gains its first gay theater company
description: The company SCHWUPS begins producing plays with gay themes in Zurich.

title: The group KLICK forms for the Zurich Oberland
description: Growing out of the Rapperswil film days, KLICK emerges in February as a gay group serving the Zurich Oberland and the upper Lake Zurich area.

title: A support group for relatives of homosexual people is founded
description: On 6 February, the group Angehörige homosexueller Menschen (AHM) is founded to support family members of lesbians and gay men.

title: Zurich hosts the seventh European lesbian and gay choral festival
description: From 19 to 23 May, choirs from across Europe gather in Zurich for the seventh European lesbian and gay choral festival.

title: Festival choirs hold a service in Zurich's Grossmünster
description: On 23 May, as part of the choral festival, singers hold a musical morning celebration in the Grossmünster, one of Zurich's principal churches.

title: Pink Cross is founded as the national umbrella for gay organizations
description: On 5 June, Swiss gay organizations found Pink Cross, a national umbrella body that also operates a national secretariat.

title: A national gay history archive is founded in Bern
description: On 10 July, the association sas is created in Bern to collect and preserve the records of gay life in Switzerland.

title: A signature drive for equal rights for same-sex couples begins
description: The campaign group Gleiche Rechte für gleichgeschlechtliche Paare starts collecting signatures for a petition demanding equal legal treatment of same-sex couples.

title: Geneva's gay men's chorus Choeur homogène is founded
description: Singers in Geneva establish the Choeur homogène, a chorus for gay men.

title: Club Labyrinth opens in Zurich
description: The Labyrinth opens first in a cellar near Albisriederplatz and soon moves, becoming the first club in Zurich's industrial quarter.
